title: NOC 75211 – Railway and motor transport labourers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 75211 professionals are the backbone of Canada’s physical distribution network. As a TEER 5 occupation, these labourers perform the essential manual work required to maintain railway tracks and support the operation of motor transport fleets. In 2026, as Canada executes its High-Frequency Rail (HFR) project between Toronto and Quebec City and expands its green logistics hubs, these workers are classified as Essential Infrastructure Talent. For immigration, this role is a key target for the Saskatchewan Hard-to-Fill Skills Pilot and the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level5Major Group75 – Helpers and labourers and other transport drivers, operators and labourersSub-Major Group752 – Other transport drivers, operators and related occupationsMinor Group7521 – Boat and cable ferry operators and related occupationsNOC Code75211

duties: Railway Maintenance: Clean and lubricate switches, help replace and repair track rails and sleepers, and clear snow from tracks and switches using shovels and specialized blowers.
Loading & Unloading: Manually load and unload goods and materials onto and off trucks, trailers, or railway cars.
Site Preparation: Dig holes and trenches for railway signals and assist in the installation of track-side equipment.
Support Tasks: Assist truck drivers and railway crews by providing manual labor for securing loads, placing ramps, and operating small hand-held power tools.
Maintenance: Perform general cleaning and maintenance of transport terminals, warehouses, and railway yards to ensure a safe work environment.
Safety: Adhere to strict railway and road safety protocols, including wearing high-visibility gear and using signaling equipment during track-side operations.
employment_requirements: No formal education is typically required.
On-the-Job Training: Extensive on-the-job training is provided by major employers (e.g., CN Rail, CPKC, or large trucking firms).

Certifications (Role Specific):Railway Safety Training is mandatory for all track-side workers.WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is a standard safety requirement.A valid driver's license is often preferred but not always mandatory for entry-level labor roles.
Railway Safety Training is mandatory for all track-side workers.
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is a standard safety requirement.
A valid driver's license is often preferred but not always mandatory for entry-level labor roles.
Physical strength, stamina for heavy lifting (up to 50 lbs), and the ability to work outdoors in all Canadian weather conditions are essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, labourers working for major national railways (CN/CPKC) often earn annual salaries between $60,000 and $78,000 when factoring in comprehensive union benefits and overtime premiums.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate that the shift toward rail-based logistics—as a more carbon-efficient alternative to long-haul trucking—will sustain high demand for track maintenance and terminal support. According to COPS, "Replacement Demand" due to the aging workforce in the rail sector will create significant job openings for international workers through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for transport labourers in 2026 is Stable to Strong, particularly in the Prairies and Central Canada. In Saskatchewan and Manitoba, the heavy reliance on rail for grain and mineral exports ensures a constant need for track maintenance crews. In Ontario and Quebec, the surge in intermodal freight (truck-to-rail) has increased the demand for labourers at inland ports and logistics terminals. According to 2026 industry projections, the sector is currently seeking over 5,000 new entrants to support upcoming track expansion projects.
Massive Infrastructure Investment: Contribute to the largest rail modernization projects in Canadian history, including high-speed corridor developments.
Rapid Employment Entry: Start your career in the transport sector with no formal education requirements and gain access to stable, unionized environments.
Pathways to Skilled Trades: Many labourers transition into high-paying roles as Railway Yardworkers, Track Maintenance Foremen, or Heavy Equipment Operators.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 5 role is a primary beneficiary of Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP) that prioritize "Essential Supply Chain" workers.
pathways: Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority stream for railway and transport labourers with a valid job offer in the province.
New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, targeting "Essential Supply Chain and Infrastructure" roles.
Manitoba Skilled Worker Overseas: Frequently awards points for experience in the transport and logistics sectors.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for those with a job offer from a designated transport employer in an Atlantic province.
